diagrams.title: Memory Hierarchy Pyramid
diagrams.description: Shows tiered arrangement from fastest/smallest to slowest/largest
diagrams.purpose: Visualize speed-capacity trade-off and understand why caching is essential

diagrams.title: Cache Address Structure
diagrams.description: Shows how memory address is divided for cache lookup
elements: Example: 32-bit address with 8KB cache, 64B block, 4-way set associative
diagrams.purpose: Understand how cache locates data and role of each address field

diagrams.title: Cache Hit vs Miss Flowchart
diagrams.description: Decision process for CPU memory request
elements: Check valid bit and tag comparison
Miss: fetch from memory, update cache, return data
diagrams.purpose: Comprehend cache operation and performance implications

diagrams.title: Write-Through vs Write-Back
elements: Write-Through: Write to cache and memory always
Write-Back: Write to cache only, memory updated on eviction
diagrams.purpose: Understand data consistency and performance trade-offs
